title: SeaweedFS
description: Fast distributed storage system for blobs, objects, files and a data lake, with an S3-compatible API and a filer web UI.
installation: Runs as an all-in-one node via the `server` command (master + volume + filer), with the S3 gateway enabled. The filer web UI is on container port 8888; the S3-compatible API is on port 8333 and the master UI on 9333 (expose internally as needed). All metadata and data persist under /data (default leveldb filer store) so no external database add-on is required. For production scale, split master/volume/filer into separate deployments and point the filer at an external metadata store. There is no built-in auth on the filer UI by default; place it behind the ingress/auth layer and configure S3 access keys before exposing the S3 endpoint.
